title: Missi Roti

overview.description: A rustic North Indian flatbread made with wholemeal atta and gram flour, seasoned with onion, herbs and spices and cooked on a hot tawa.

ingredients: Extra oil or ghee, for cooking
method: Mix the atta, gram flour, onion, coriander, green chilli if using, cumin, ajwain, turmeric and salt in a large bowl.
Gradually add the water, mixing until a soft dough forms.
Knead gently until the dough comes together and is reasonably smooth.
Divide the dough into 6 equal portions and roll each into a ball.
Dust one ball with flour and roll into a roughly circular flatbread.
Heat a tawa or heavy frying pan over medium-high heat.
Place the roti on the hot pan.
Cook until the surface begins to develop bubbles and the underside has brown spots.
Turn and brush lightly with oil or ghee.
Turn again and brush the other side.
Cook until golden-brown spots appear and the roti is cooked through.

learning.did-you-know: Missi roti is particularly associated with North Indian cuisine and is traditionally made with gram flour mixed with wheat flour, giving it a distinctive savoury flavour and texture.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to combine wheat flour and gram flour in a dough and use spices, onion and herbs to create a savoury flatbread.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A savoury flatbread that introduces the children to gram flour, ajwain and a different style of seasoned Indian bread.
chapman-tips: Chop the onion very finely so it does not tear the dough while rolling.
Ajwain has a strong flavour, so a little goes a long way.
The dough may feel slightly rougher than a normal chapati dough because of the gram flour.
If the dough cracks while rolling, knead in a little more water.
Keep the heat high enough to brown the roti but not so high that the onion burns.
Try one without chilli first, then add chilli in future batches if the family enjoys it.
notes.batch-cooking: Missi roti freezes well. Cool completely, stack with baking paper between the rotis if desired and freeze in an airtight bag or container. Reheat directly from frozen in a dry frying pan.
notes.leftovers: Use as a packed lunch wrap, tear into pieces for dipping or reheat and serve with eggs.
